task_completion.description: The diagram contains exactly 2 XOR gates, 2 AND gates, and 1 OR gate.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the draw.io canvas for the distinct shapes of logic gates: exactly two XOR gates (curved input side with a double line, pointed output), two AND gates (flat input side, rounded D-shape output), and one OR gate (curved input side, pointed output).

task_completion.description: The diagram includes text labels for all required inputs and outputs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for text boxes placed near the input and output wires on the canvas reading 'A', 'B', 'Cin' (inputs) and 'Sum', 'Cout' (outputs).

task_completion.description: The gates are wired correctly according to the 'Two Half-Adders' topology.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Trace the connecting lines: Inputs A and B should connect to the first XOR gate and the first AND gate. The output of the first XOR gate and the Cin input should connect to the second XOR gate (outputting Sum) and the second AND gate. The outputs of both AND gates should connect to the inputs of the OR gate (outputting Cout).

task_completion.description: The diagram is saved as 'full_adder.drawio' in the '~/Diagrams/' directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the draw.io window title showing 'full_adder.drawio', a save dialog confirming the path '~/Diagrams/full_adder.drawio', or a file manager window showing the file in the Diagrams folder.

task_completion.description: The diagram is exported as a PNG image to '~/Diagrams/exports/full_adder.png'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an export dialog in draw.io saving to '~/Diagrams/exports/full_adder.png', or a file manager window showing 'full_adder.png' inside the 'exports' subdirectory.

integrity.description: The agent used the Diagrams.net (draw.io) graphical interface to create the circuit.
integrity.visual_evidence: The draw.io application window should be visible in the screenshots with the component sidebar, grid canvas, and toolbars actively being used.

integrity.description: The agent manually placed and wired the components rather than just opening a pre-existing image.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for intermediate steps showing gates being dragged onto the canvas, selected components with resize/connect handles, or active drawing of connecting wires.

integrity.description: The exported PNG file visually matches the circuit drawn in the draw.io canvas.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final exported image (if opened or previewed) should be an exact visual representation of the gate-level schematic built in the draw.io application.

privileged_info_for_vlm: A standard 1-bit full adder using the 'Two Half-Adders' topology requires exactly 5 gates: 2 XOR, 2 AND, and 1 OR. The required labels are A, B, Cin, Sum, and Cout. The correct wiring logic is: Sum = (A XOR B) XOR Cin; Cout = (A AND B) OR (Cin AND (A XOR B)). Visually, this means A and B go into the first XOR and AND. The first XOR's output and Cin go into the second XOR (which outputs Sum) and the second AND. The outputs of both AND gates go into the OR gate (which outputs Cout).
